#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# setup.sh - Full setup for a BRAND NEW codespace
# Run this once after cloning:
#   git clone https://github.com/Walusimbi-Leon1/Codespace-3.git
#   cd Codespace-3
#   chmod +x setup.sh && ./setup.sh

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
START_TIME=$(date +%s)

echo "=================================================="
echo "  🐾 L4 Claw Pack - Setup"
echo "  Setting up a fresh codespace..."
echo "  $(date)"
echo "=================================================="
echo ""

# ── Step 1: Prerequisites ──────────────────────────────────
echo "📦 Step 1/6: Checking prerequisites..."

# Node.js & npm
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  ❌ Node.js not found. Install it first."
  exit 1
fi
echo "  ✅ Node.js $(node -v)"

if ! command -v npm &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  ❌ npm not found."
  exit 1
fi
echo "  ✅ npm $(npm -v)"

# Git
if ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  echo "  ❌ Git not found."
  exit 1
fi
echo "  ✅ Git $(git --version | awk '{print $3}')"

# Set pull.rebase to false for clean pulls
git config pull.rebase false 2>/dev/null || true
echo "  ✅ Git pull.rebase = false"

echo ""

# ── Step 2: Global npm packages ────────────────────────────
echo "📦 Step 2/6: Checking global npm packages..."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Start 9-router (AI model router)
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
echo "🌐 9-router: checking..."

if pgrep -f "node.*9router" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  PID=$(pgrep -f "node.*9router" | head -1)
  echo "  ✅ 9-router already running (PID $PID)"
else
  echo "  Starting 9-router..."
  nohup 9router --port 20128 &>/tmp/9router.log &
  sleep 2
  if pgrep -f "node.*9router" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  ✅ 9-router started on port 20128"
  else
    echo "  ❌ 9-router failed to start - check /tmp/9router.log"
  fi
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Start 9-router (AI model router)
# NOTE: Pin to 9router@0.5.8 — 0.5.12 crashes on codespaces (Next.js exits instantly)
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
echo "🌐 9-router: checking..."

if pgrep -f "node.*9router" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  PID=$(pgrep -f "node.*9router" | head -1)
  echo "  ✅ 9-router already running (PID $PID)"
else
  echo "  Starting 9-router..."
  # --tray: headless mode (no TTY needed)
  # --skip-update: avoid hanging on update check
  # --host 127.0.0.1: local-only, no network exposure warning
  nohup 9router --port 20128 --host 127.0.0.1 --tray --skip-update &>/tmp/9router.log &
  sleep 4
  if pgrep -f "node.*9router" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  ✅ 9-router started on port 20128"
  else
    echo "  ❌ 9-router failed to start - check /tmp/9router.log"
    cat /tmp/9router.log 2>/dev/null
  fi
fi
